rationale_for_baseline_tier: A plantar wart is benign and managed with topical salicylic acid, filing and offloading padding plus podiatry, with routine review (T1). Secondary infection / diabetic-foot problems route to GP/ED (T4); atypical/non-resolving lesions route to GP for biopsy.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The area becoming red, hot, swollen or leaking pus, a red streak up the foot or a fever (infection); or an atypical, pigmented, bleeding, rapidly-growing or non-resolving lesion; or foot problems in a diabetic
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day (infection/diabetic foot) / prompt GP (atypical lesion)
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary infection / diabetic-foot needs urgent care; an atypical lesion needs biopsy to exclude malignancy.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the foot becomes red, hot, swollen or leaks pus, you get a red streak or fever, or the lump looks unusual, changes colour, bleeds on its own or won't clear — see a doctor (that day if it looks infected).

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; fall risk from the antalgic gait
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Offloading padding relieves pain and reduces fall risk; give infection/malignancy red flags.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Paring and a padded 'donut' relieve the pain straight away. Use the salicylic-acid treatment daily after filing the hard skin, wear thongs in shared showers, and see a podiatrist for freezing/paring. The wart takes weeks to clear.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + podiatry in 1–2 weeks; GP/ED for infection/diabetic foot; GP for atypical lesions
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: GP/ED for a hot, red, painful, pus-leaking foot or a red streak/fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Verruca plantaris; corn/malignancy/infection/diabetic-foot screened negative; salicylic acid + paring/offloading + podiatry; biopsy if atypical/non-resolving.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A plantar melanoma or a diabetic-foot infection is mislabelled as a wart and progresses.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not distinguishing from a corn / no footwear-hygiene advice.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED for a simple painful wart — unnecessary (podiatry/self-care).
